perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York applicable to agreements made and to be performed in that state, without regard to any of its principles of conflicts of laws or other laws that would result in the application of the laws of another jurisdiction. This Agreement shall be construed and interpreted without regard to any presumption against the party causing this Agreement to be drafted. Each of the parties hereby unconditionally and irrevocably waives the right to a trial by jury in any action, suit or proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement or the transactions contemplated hereby. Each of the parties unconditionally and irrevocably consents to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of the State of New York located in the County of New York and the Federal district court for the Southern District of New York located in the County of New York with respect to any suit, action or proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement or the transactions contemplated hereby, and each of the parties hereby unconditionally and irrevocably waives any objection to venue in any such court.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York applicable to agreements made and to be performed in that state, without regard to any of its principles of conflicts of laws or other laws that would result in the application of the laws of another jurisdiction. This Agreement shall be construed and interpreted without regard to any presumption against the party causing this Agreement to be drafted. Each of the parties unconditionally and irrevocably consents to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of the State of New York located in the County of New York and the Federal district court for the Southern District of New York located in the County of New York with respect to any suit, action or proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement or the transactions contemplated hereby, and each of the parties hereby unconditionally and irrevocably waives any objection to venue in any such court. Each of the parties hereby waives the right to a trial by jury in any action, suit or proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement or the transactions contemplated hereby.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the waiver of jury trial clause makes it less prominent and harder to find, potentially hindering its enforceability.
perturbation.contradicted_law: 7th Amendment to the United States Constitution
perturbation.law_explanation: While the 7th Amendment guarantees the right to a jury trial in civil cases, this right can be waived. However, waivers must be knowing and voluntary. By obscuring the jury trial waiver within the general provisions and separating it from other legal declarations, it could be argued that the waiver was not conspicuously presented, thus making it less likely that the waiving party understood the implications and therefore less likely the waiver was knowing and voluntary. This undermines the protection provided by the 7th Amendment.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Seventh Amendment | U.S. Constitution | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Seventh Amendment The Seventh Amendment continues a practice from English common law of distinguishing civil claims which must be tried before a jury (absent waiver by the parties) from claims and issues that may be heard by a judge alone. It only governs federal civil courts and has no application to civil courts set up by the states when those courts are hearing only disputes of state law . Learn more... Primary tabs Amendment VII In suits at common law, where the value in controversy shall exceed twenty dollars, the right of trial by jury shall be preserved, and no fact tried by a jury, shall be otherwise reexamined in any court of the United States, than according to the rules of the common law. Wex Resources Criminal Law Criminal Procedure Jury Other Resources US Code: Title 18 Federal Rules of Criminal Procedure ‹ Sixth Amendment up Eighth Amendment › U.S. Constitution Toolbox Explanation of the Constitution - from the Congressional Research Service
perturbation.justification: The modification involves a structural flaw by moving the jury trial waiver clause, potentially hindering its enforceability. The Law Explanation argues that this obscures the waiver, making it less likely to be knowing and voluntary, thus undermining the 7th Amendment. Scraped Law Snippet 2 mentions that the right to a jury trial exists "absent waiver by the parties". By making the waiver less conspicuous, the changed text creates a legal contradiction, as a less prominent waiver could be argued as not being a valid waiver, thereby contradicting the condition under which the right to a jury trial can be waived.

perturbation.original_text: If any action for breach of or to enforce the provisions of this Agreement is commenced, the court in such action shall award to the party in whose favor a judgment is entered, a reasonable sum as attorneys' fees and costs. Such attorneys' fees and costs shall be paid by the non-prevailing party in such action.
perturbation.changed_text: If any action for breach of or to enforce the provisions of this Agreement is commenced, the court in such action may award to the party in whose favor a judgment is entered, a reasonable sum as attorneys' fees and costs. Such attorneys' fees and costs may be paid by the non-prevailing party in such action.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall' to 'may' weakens the mandatory nature of awarding attorneys' fees to the prevailing party.
perturbation.contradicted_law: New York law principles of contract interpretation and enforcement
perturbation.law_explanation: Under New York law, contracts are to be interpreted to give effect to the parties' intent. When a contract uses the word 'shall,' it generally indicates a mandatory obligation. Changing 'shall' to 'may' introduces ambiguity regarding the awarding of attorneys' fees, potentially undermining the parties' original intent and weakening the enforceability of the provision. By introducing optionality where there was previously a clear obligation, this creates uncertainty and a departure from the expected legal outcome.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: NYS Open Legislation | NYSenate.gov Sorry, you need to enable JavaScript to visit this website. Skip to main content The requested entry could not be found. Try searching OpenLegislation Browse all Laws of New York
perturbation.justification: The change from 'shall' to 'may' significantly weakens the obligation to award attorneys' fees. The original text mandates the court to award fees, whereas the changed text gives the court discretion. The LLM-generated Law Explanation accurately notes that 'shall' typically indicates a mandatory obligation in contracts, and changing it introduces ambiguity, potentially undermining the parties' original intent. This falls under Structural Flaws - Legal Contradiction, where the enforceability is affected. While the snippets provided are unhelpful, the legal principle that 'shall' creates a mandatory obligation is well-established, and the perturbation explanation confirms the intent was to weaken the mandatory nature. The modified clause weakens the contractual term, making it difficult to enforce.

perturbation.original_text: The provisions of this Agreement shall be deemed severable and if any portion hereof shall be held invalid, illegal or unenforceable for any reason by a court of competent jurisdiction, the remainder shall not thereby be invalidated but shall remain in full force and effect.
perturbation.changed_text: The provisions of this Agreement shall be deemed severable only if the core restrictive covenants remain enforceable, and if any other portion hereof shall be held invalid, illegal or unenforceable for any reason by a court of competent jurisdiction, the remainder shall not thereby be invalidated but shall remain in full force and effect.
perturbation.explanation: Adding a condition related to restrictive covenants makes it difficult to determine if the original intention of the severability clause will be maintained.
perturbation.contradicted_law: General principles of contract law regarding severability
perturbation.law_explanation: The Restatement (Second) of Contracts § 184 outlines the principles of severability in contracts. By adding the condition regarding the enforceability of 'core restrictive covenants,' the altered text narrows the application of the severability clause. This means that if a court finds a provision other than a core restrictive covenant to be invalid, the remainder of the agreement's enforceability is still preserved as long as the core restrictive covenants are valid. However, if core restrictive covenants are deemed invalid, there is increased potential for invalidation of the whole agreement. This introduces a level of contingency that may be challenged as undermining the original intention and reasonable expectations of the parties, since the whole agreement now has a greater dependency on single sections for overall survival.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: contract | Wex | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you contract A contract is an agreement between parties , creating mutual obligations that are enforceable by law . The basic elements required for the agreement to be a legally enforceable contract are: mutual assent , expressed by a valid offer and acceptance ; adequate consideration ; capacity ; and legality . In some states , elements of consideration can be satisfied by a valid substitute. Possible remedies for breach of contract include general damages , consequential damages , reliance damages , and specific performance . Background : Contracts are promises that the law will enforce. Contract law is generally governed by state common law , and while general overall contract law is common throughout the country, some specific court interpretations of a particular element of the contract may vary between the states. If a promise is breached, the law provides remedies to the harmed party, often in the form of monetary damages, or in limited circumstances, in the form of specific performance of the promise made. Elements -- Consideration and Mutual Assent Contracts arise when a duty comes into existence, because of a promise made by one of the parties. To be legally binding as a contract, a promise must be exchanged for adequate consideration. There are two different theories or definitions of consideration: Bargain Theory of Consideration and Benefit-Detriment theory of consideration. Benefit-Detriment Under the b enefit-detriment theory, an adequate consideration exists only when a promise made to the benefit of the promisor or to the detriment of the promisee, which reasonably and fairly induces the promisor to make a promise for something else for the promisee. For example, promises that are purely gifts are not considered enforceable because the personal satisfaction the grantor of the promise may receive from the act of generosity is normally not considered sufficient detriment to constitute adequate consideration. Bargain-for-Exchange Under Bargain-for-Exchange theory of consideration, adequate consideration exists when a promisor makes a promise in return for something else. Here, the essential condition is that the promisor was given something specifically to induce the promise being made. In other words, the bargain for exchange theory is different from the detriment-benefit theory in that the focus in bargain for exchange theory seems to be the parties’ motive for making the promises and the parties’ subjective mutual assent, while in detriment benefit theory, the focus seems to be an objective legal detriment or benefit to the parties. Governing Laws Contracts are mainly governed by state statutory and common (judge-made) law and private law (i.e. the private agreement). Private law principally includes the terms of the agreement between the parties who are exchanging promises. This private law may override many of the rules otherwise established by state law. Statutory law, such as the Statute of Fraud , may require some kinds of contracts be put in writing and executed with particular formalities, for the contract to be enforceable. Otherwise, the parties may enter into a binding agreement without signing a formal written document. For example, Virginia Supreme Court has held in Lucy v. Zehmer that even an agreement made on a piece of napkin can be considered a valid contract, if the parties were both sane, and showed mutual assent and consideration. Most of the principles of the common law of contracts are outlined in the Restatement of Law, Second Contracts published by the American Law Institute. The Uniform Commercial Code , whose original articles have been adopted in nearly every state, represents a body of statutory law that governs important categories of contracts. The main articles that deal with the law of contracts are Article 1 (General Provisions) and Article 2 (Sales) . Sections of Article 9 (
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a condition related to 'core restrictive covenants' that narrows the application of the severability clause. This undermines the original intention of maintaining the agreement's enforceability, creating a contingency where the entire agreement's survival depends on specific sections. This is a structural flaw because the change affects the clarity and enforceability of the contract's severability provisions. The Law Explanation also states that if core restrictive covenants are deemed invalid, there is an increased potential for invalidation of the whole agreement. Scraped Law Snippet 2 explains that contracts create mutual obligations that are enforceable by law, and the added condition impacts that enforceability, making the agreement's outcome less certain and more contingent on the core restrictive covenants.
